reviews.review_text:
My First MH game. I tried Rise years ago and gave up as it felt very complicated. This time around I made my mind and finally started. It's been a long journey Almost took me 200 hours to complete Iceborne and even now it fells like I have more than 200 hours worth of stuff left. I am definitely going for all achievements in this game.
As for gameplay , It's PEAK, everything down to the last minute detail. So much love is poured into developing this combat system it blows my mind. There are so many weapon choices I haven't even tried half myself. I thought I finished the game and the game throws another region in with more harder monsters for endgame, I am definitely gonna do everything this game has to offer. All the monsters are remarkable and interesting its just amazing to see. Also this game is not for the 'movie' game fans only people who enjoy games for what they are which is a nice filter I guess. I'll update the review when I am done with all the stuff.

I guess I'll leave a positive review.

The main issue with this game is the way you often miss and get locked in animations, which can ruin your fun a lot of times. But when you learn to land hits, craft builds, and adjust the menus to yourself, you get a satisfying experience.

Honestly, this is one of the best games I have ever played.  For context I have over 300 hours on the xbox and am just starting new with a PC account.  You don't play it much for the story, but the combat.  Oh the combat.  Its so great to build an understanding of a monster's attack patterns and then be able to fight it like a well choreographed  dance.  Honestly, if you are newer to Monster Hunter in general this and Monster hunter rise are great games to start with.
First game from the Monster Hunter franchise, loved the base game and also the DLC.
fun game but only if you have the iceborn dlc for the grappling hook it changes the game drastically
Best Monster Hunter, great graphics, great gameplay.
Fire game. Holds up well on a mid range laptop like mine averaging 80 fps. Just a good experience overall.

Had a streak of months where I would play this game for HOURS, and that was before I got Iceborne and the moment I do my hours tripled. Incredibly fun, incredibly challenging, with the gameplay, music, and monster designs being absolutely top tier.

I don't regret a single hour I spent on this game, I still listen to the OSTs from time to time, it's that good.

Great game and got it on sale. Played some previous entries of the franchise before this, got rise, still playing this, still great even years after its launch. Would recommend unless you hate learning and value time because it's grindy as hell (you will learn real love)

Bless you gamers

good game
if you like souls-like games but dont enjoy their very hard difficulty, you will definitely enjoy this one.
you should also buy its DLC, the content is very good and can last you years of playing

When you get into the game, you get to hunt the monter. When you hun t the monter, the monter gets hunted. I like hunting the monter cause when you hunt the monter, you get the monter. hunting the monter is fun. 
| : )

Don't get Monster Hunter Wilds, buy this instead. 

It is superior in every way - including graphics.
begging on my knees for the devs to make more DLC and expansions for this game instead of Wilds
Very Good. Amazing story and gameplay
I feel I might like the game if I were more into dinosaurs as a little kid?  Kinda neat to try out but the combat feels unnaturally slow and very quickly repititive.
its actually really fun,if you love combat and exploring this is for you
Probably the worst possible experience in any game ever. This is an actual scam.

An outstanding game from an art direction standpoint. Both the monsters and weapons are iconic; there is so much variety and freshness in both it's outstanding. I had so much fun with the gameplay loop of strategising for the next fight, and then taking up to 40min to slowly chip away at a tough boss, overcoming these beasts as the great challenge they're presented to be. This effectively makes the monsters feel every bit as powerful as the lore suggests.

My only grievance is how much grinding is woven into its gameplay loop. I love strategising and fighting new bosses, but the requirement of repeating old fights over and over to get items, which can have up to a 1% drop rate, so I can then effectively fight the monsters I actually want to fight was unengaging. I don't like grinding, so I wish there was an option to lessen its importance in the gameplay loop because, at its best, this game is amazing. Even so, I love this game and it is an easy recommendation.
It's been some hours and I am so much addictive to this game ! Man the early game monster is really impressive and challenging, can't wait to see what comes next XD

INCREDIBLY SLOW. Almost had a heart attack trying to walk around and kill two raptors with a goofy ass katana that weights five hundred kilos. I never get the appeal of MH and I never will. Good try though.

Monster Hunter: World is one of those games that keeps pulling me back in. The grind for new weapons and armor never feels boring because every hunt feels like progress toward your next goal. There's always that excitement of finally getting the rare material you've been chasing and crafting the gear you've wanted for hours.

This game has a fluid combat system, many weapons that feel diffrent, and is overall fun!
still hate the one girl that gives you the quests...
Comparing this game to the older ones is just mean, on its own it's great, could be better with the G rank expansion. but besides that, it's fine

After playing MH3U for 1200 hours, MH4U for 600 hours, and MH Rise for 200, I wasn't sure if I had the time or will to do another one. I have been thoroughly impressed and happily surprised to find that World is both 1) worth playing even if you've done much of Rise's content (I plan on putting 500-600 more hours to 100% that game) and 2) is different enough from other entries that it feels less like a fresh coat of paint and more like a (familiar) new experience. Battles and areas are the most beautiful in the series (other than Wilds, but my PC would melt!), the story is very good for a Monster Hunter entry, and there is some depth to other systems that are different from previous generations.
big fan, liked the game since the start and it only gets better

Genuinely the most fun I've ever had while playing a game. I can confidently say that out of my whole steam library, this is probably my favorite game. Up until some portions of Iceborne, every new monster felt like it was amazingly designed and it was extremely fun learning how each different monster acted, and how it fit into its environment. 
Iceborne is a bit different in that I do think that the monsters are of a worse design, but the game still stays incredibly fun. My only complaint is that every new monster becomes this unsurpassable brick wall that you have to continually throw yourself at. Baroth was easy enough, but I don't really think its fun sitting around while it constantly jumps around without stopping. Velkhana is even worse in that it just feels like its made for melee play only? I don't feel like you should ever be required to need to use I-frames in a roll to do something, it really doesn't feel natural and it really ruins immersion in my opinion. (I am a heavy bowgun user)

Again, other than that though, this game is amazing. Beautiful environments, amazing designs, and generally really fun.
Great for both new players as well as returning Hunters!

best game ever, its super fun to listen heavy metal or rock or songs that have a deep and heavy bass while killing the monsters.
the veriety of weapons and armor are great for all kinds of play style, whether your a heavy hitter or a lightweight who attacks weak points, theres probably a weapon youll like

arguably the definitive modern monhun game. if you can't be bothered digging through old hardware to play the classic games or have little interest in plundering treasure this is a great starting point. It's less graphically demanding than wilds (terrible game) and goes on sale with dlc for far cheaper pretty frequently.
My introduction to the franchise, and Astera's still my favorite setting.

The gameplay is top-tier. Every weapon feels heavy and impactful, and the raid system with monsters like Kulve Taroth and Safi'jiiva is just incredibly fun. Having the Gathering Hub completely packed with players during these raids is an absolute game-changer that massively boosts the overall Quality of Life (QoL) and social experience. What makes the world even better is the attention to detail. Everything feels so immersive, from the lively NPCs and Palicoes to the ecosystems in the hub towns and out in the fields, including all the small endemic life and monsters.

Monster Hunter World and I had a great time together. Then I met one specific monster, who I will not name because saying it out loud reopens the wound, and that monster killed me so many times that I quietly closed the game, uninstalled it, and pretended the whole relationship never happened. Every friend I have has since told me it "really opens up" after that fight. They say this with the glassy-eyed conviction of people who have seen something beautiful. I nod. I smile. I will never know what they know. 

10/10 for the people who pushed through, 7/10 for me, the coward.
Hands down the best Monster Hunter game I've played! Can't count the no. of hours I've sunk in this game both on my PS4 and PC.

No. i did not get this for free and i lied about it.
The game itself it worth purchasing IF there is a sale going on. I recommend it to everyone who is a fan of open world monster hunting (lmfao)

addicted on steamdeck, the best. btw the learning curve is something, 100 hour playing longsword, and just got a hang to aim the foresight slash. cant wait to master and move on to charge blade or other weird weapon.  simple game yet very rewarding

Best Monster Hunter till date. The game was way better without the clutch claw, but compared to the newer MH releases, this is still way better than the others. MH Rise comes a close second.

Played this for 600+ hrs on PS4, just bought it on Steam for the love of it + it was on sale. Really enjoyed my days with this game, very close to my heart.

Do give it a shot and GET ICEBORNE AFTER COMPLETING THE BASE GAME, DO NOT RUSH THE BASE GAME. The learning curve is pretty steep, but once it clicks, it's one of the best combat systems you'll have experienced.

I never actually intended to complete it 100%. But now that a year has passed since Dauntless was discontinued, I am doing so to honor it.
I always played World and Dauntless side by side—and I hope that Capcom or someone else brings it back.

Mission:
Day of Ruin
would 100% reccomend 2nd best monster hunter, first is wilds great amount of hunts and weapons, GS gang rise up
monter hunter good, handler hopeless, monters fun to fight. >:3c play it!

This is my first entry on MH series,  i thought I'll liked it, played it for about 32 hours total, got the fun for about 20 hours gameplay, then i cant get the catch of repetitive slow hunting and grinding anymore, maybe this is not for me. But still a good game.
My first monster hunter game, it was quite challenging because I mostly played solo. It was worth buying this for hundreds of hours.

“running around a tree world in italian” - wii gamer edmotus

But for real, the characters speak in what sounds to be either Italian or Spanish which I must have done months ago when I configured my settings and now I don’t know how to change it, lol.
It looks intimidating at first, but once you understand the fundamentals, it becomes so fun and satisfying to play.
10/10 would recommend. I wish I had the ability to wipe my own memory so I can experience this game for the first time again.
Wilds players will cope and seethe about their game being better

Oh my sweet heavens. It's incredible! My new favorite series, and go to for games. I love hunting, and hunting monsters. I'm hunting.. Dinosaur hunting >:) Mods are incredible too. You can mod just about everything. Once you learn monster attack patterns, you feel like a god when you parry and pull off counter attacks, and stagger/stumble the monsters. Soo satisfying and quick to get addicted to once you learn. I love MH franchise.
very good very nice, big weapons + big monsters = best game
Bought this game because it was on sale a while ago, suddenly became one of my top 10 games, I love everything about it and I still have a lot to learn and do after 100 hours of gameplay
Came back after bouncing off a few years ago. Loves the collection of monster parts. Satisfying progression and numbers go brr. Great on steamdeck as well.

unlocked high rank yesterday, I think this game is the best in it's genre. one of my only complains are the hitboxes and also the armor upgrading system which is good on paper but too much grind in my opinion. There's a huge gap of difficulty from low to high rank and armor spheres are essentials for progression. My verdict.. I love this game, I love the content creation and I like greatsword slop.
This game is a lot of fun. And fun fact; it helped me get good at tracking wild life in real life (played for many hours on a different account which contributed to that skill).

incredible gameplay, insanely fun, runs amazing with gorgeous graphics

i could honestly play this forever
can't stop monster hunting world at monster hunter world. there was that one time when i hunted a monster in the world and that's deep

This game is great, its not my first monster hunter game but it is still a great game. The only gripe's I have with the game is zenny is a little annoying to grind for sometimes and its not cross platform. Even then the combats great, the monsters are amazing, and the armor looks great. For a game that came out in 2018 the game has aged very well, if you like games like shadows of the colossus but multiplayer then this is great. i'd give the game a 9.5/10.

Games great until The Guiding Lands, at which point monsters just become unfun to fight in every way one could imagine.
7/10 Experience
is very fun and amazing game, if i could i would forget about it so i can experience it new all over again.
The only thing that I hate about this game is that it's just half of the game. you must pay again to play the other half. good game for wasting your hours. Don't expect anything about story.

This game is awesome! I have played some of the older games in the series, particularly the 3DS and PSP titles like Freedom 2, 3 Ultimate, and 4 Ultimate. This game is fast becoming one of my favorites. I love the connectedness and seamlessness of each map, a departure from previous games in the series that had maps segmented apart by loading screens. I also love the capture net and being able to catch a whole bunch of cute animals for my room. My few complaints are that the story is okay at best, and that the game is always online, even for single player. The story is serviceable, but not great by any means, and the online connectivity requirement is annoying, but not too big of a deal for me personally. The gameplay is what really shines in this game, and it is really fun. Any fan of Monster Hunter should pick this up, and even those new to the series will likely have a really good time with this one (once they get past the initial barrier to entry the series has). This is an easy recommendation from me.

pretty tight. one of the best monster hunters. lots of content, completion takes 100s of hours. biggest gripe is the messy multiplayer. everybody else already talked about it so ill just tell you my solution; if you want to play an alright singleplayer story mode play this, if you want to play mainly multiplayer and dont care about singleplayer play monhunt rise instead. overall highly recommended.
Si eres un fan de la serie este es un gran cambio a un gameplay mas moderno, la 5ta gen se siente mas agil y rapido pero tamien saisfactorio.

If you are searching for a game where:
- An evil pickle will always invade you at the worst time ever
- You have a cat pet that ONLY heals you after you fully healed yourself
- A very angy monkee likes to give free hugs
- Another very very very angy monkee giving a proof that Frieza had a point
- Oh the cat ONLY revives you if you are in a position that ENSURES you will die again RIGHT AFTER being revived too
- There is literally a bomber plane that following you whenever you go
- You will be lost in the only 2 main hub areas after the 6942067th time
- A chicken with a blue rock is scarier than a "biblically accurate angel" dragon
- The other players are attacking you rather than the monster
- A pointy birb who screams non stop like your wife
- A VERY ENTERTAINING AND SO MUCH FUN escort mission is waiting for you
- There are big cats/cars that like to honk and drift (toward you btw)
- Oh did I already tell you that the cat is supposed to save you when you pass out or paralyzed? Emphasis on the word "supposed"

Then this game might be for you.
a great game with the right kind of challenge. fun solo or with friends
Just yes its amazing and i am saying nothing but that if you talk about this game + its makes every fight fell like a boss fight
i enjoy this game i just beaten xeno jiiva after 60 hours now i can finally play the game it also ran well at 2k res running with 110 fps most of the time

Jako moja pierwsza gra z gatunku Monster Hunter, bardzo mi się spodobała. Różnorodność broni jest bardzo zadowalająca, jest dużo typów broni więc każdy może sobie znaleźć broń pod swój playstyle. Polowania na potwory są wolne, ale satysfakcjonujące. 
Jednak jak każdy wie, wszędzie znajdą się też minusy. Na początku postacie (poza cutscenami) są nieme, co było trudne do przyzwyczajenia się. Multiplayers jest trudny to zrozumienia na początek. Nie ma per-se Lobby tylko jest dołączanie do misji innych osób poprzez sygnały SOS. 
Z grubsza bardzo polecam, ale nie jest to gra dla wszyskich. Mam nadzieje, że przydała się opinia.
Addictive and fun boss rush, hunting monsters for better and better gear. So much content!!! amazing game
My favourite monster hunter game by far. it's a 10/10 in everything for me. sounds, effects, graphics, combat, diversity of weapons and monsters. HUGE recommendation.
i like thee gameplay the mechanics and the monsters and especially the armors

Thought wouldn't enjoy it but the complexity, and sand box this game is and the accessibility for online help make this game so wonderful. I love building set and planning my next hunt! what a game :D

game started to lag and stutter too much, it's been two days, i have tried everything i can find on the internet.
it just started randomly when opened the game after 2 days, it's completely unplayable right, it was very smooth and playable up until that lag started, i hope the high texture pack helps other wise im not killing fatalis i guess.

update: the high texture pack did the magic and it running smooth
My first introduction into the monster hunter series. Loved getting up close to the ecology and hitting it with a hammer.
This game so good, it has a good mechanism, story and gameplay.
One of my absolute favorite grindy games.. Adding friends to the mix makes it 10x more fun
Look, this ain't old-school Monster Hunter. But ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ did this game take over my life when it came out.

I have hundreds of hours of Monster Hunter World on both the Playstation as well as PC. I've loved Monster Hunter since it's release in my childhood.

This is by far the best title I've ever played, and will continue playing! Even if it's hours of being on an expedition with a friend or three, the game has so much to it- I highly recommend this game.

I was really excited for this game but it just doesn't click for me. 

Combat is awkward, controls feel outdated, hunting a monster becomes repetitive and boring once you are only doing it over and over again for the loot. Plus, I am told that I should spend HOURS with a weapon in the training area to get a "feel" for the weapon. No. Either it clicks or it doesn't. 

Now, the monsters themselves and the area are absolutely gorgeous. I truly didn't get tired of the same map over and over again because there were so many spots I could crawl into only to emerge at an almost underground like system or places I could climb up and up until I was at the very top and it felt amazing. With each visit, I found new things to adore. It had so much depth to it, literally, and I couldn't get enough. This is how a forest SHOULD feel like.

If the gameplay was fun for me, I'm sure I would have loved it. But the way it is right now, I can't bring myself to continue. Maybe the other game will appeal to me.

40 hours in can finally play with my friends!

It would be cool if you could play the entire game with friends without having to deal with the cutscenes, solo missions and the SOS to co-op for most of the campaign. Still a great game though really cool.

After playing this game for the first time on the Xbox One as a wee lad, I can say coming back to this game older, more experienced and still ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ but good enough to get through some hunts have been amazing. I still absolutely love this game and I think this will go down as my favorite Monster Hunter, no questions asked.

The best looking, mechanic wise, dopamine rush, unexpected, crazy, boss fights i've ever fought. The fact that every monster alone is insane, and the fact that some time you will be fighting more than one is crazy
Great co-op gameplay loop and awesome open-world environments to explore.
played base world, loved it, i recommend you dont use the guardian set because it'll ruin ur playthrough if you plan on buying mhwi, anyways 9/10 experience, buying iceborne next!

Can be tough to get into, with some control and Ui choices that are... let's say not exactly industry standard.
But the game is a genuine masterpiece, so it's well worth learning.

First title I've ever played of Monster Hunter and I can easily say that it's a absolute Master piece. I've played it before in my playstation for more than 500h and after that I really got into the franchise, played MH Stories and Rise and through my whole experience I concluded that the Monster Hunter World is one of the best games of the franchise to get the know about the world of Monster Hunter.

 Anyways, about the game, it can run decently in the average specs of nowadays computers, the community is pretty much active, playing the game with friends or solo is a lot of fun, it has a good amount of content to be explored and the whole progression of skill and equipment upgrade is a really nice experience, I've felt rewarded with each achievement and overall I can resume the game as a good "End of a rough day reliever", like, 30 mins of gameplay before going to bed kind of thing. 

 In conclusion, since I can say that it had been a part of my life now, I'm totally biased about recommending it, but when rating it using parameters like replayability, hardware requirements (according to what the game deliver) and game content, to me it's a solid 8/10, totally worth a try!!!
This is a good game once you pass the first phase, It is complicated put worth the grind and really does test ones skills.

it is really good game and i have a lot lot of fun with it, despite it is noob friendly, it has a really high skill ceiling, and the tools you have to use it properly to make use of it. the amount of content, even in base game is alot, so if you're broke and only have the base game, it is still really good
i remember playing this game when i was younger, and i absolutely loved it. i bought it again and got addicted again lol. iceborne is very worth it, would recommend

While I can Play the game just fine on my Steam Deck,  For some reason It just does not run on my computer.  Object stretching, Infinite loading screens and Frequent crashing are the problems, and whenever I try to start the game again I get an error message saying "E_INVALIDARG: IDX11Device->CreatePixelShader(pcode,code_size,nullptr,&pps)".  I would love to play this game on my computer but it just does not work.  I have looked several solutions and even ask the customer support and still nothing
It’s fine, but growing up with Monster Hunter on the PSP, Rise feels more like the MH games of old.

To many players this was an introduction to the series.

It has everything for me to love this game but I just can't bring myself to enjoy it. 
Tedious, grindy, constantly repeats itself, every quest is just another farm chore, time limits so be sure to finish every boring thing this game has to offer.

I like the idea a lot, the preparation gameplay, designs, animations and monster interactions but it just turns into a job in the end. Fail to meet your 50 minute deadline and you get punished by grinding more to get the chance to work on your task again.

The grind in this game exists so you could grind more. Pick up random stuff off the floor, follow the blinky thing, kill kill kill all the fauna then go report to your base.

Forgot about the story completely, it's an Exposition-Dump City. This game would genuinely become better if it didn't have story at all. Like you have to make an effort to write something that dull.

If not for my friend I would have ditched this game long time ago. Like I already did 2 times.

As someone who has played the game from beginning to end I can say for sure it is my least favorite in the series. Despite all my hours put in a majority are from the most tedious grinding in the series. No other game in the series before and after is as bad this one in particular when it comes to collecting materials and upgrading equipment. Is it any wonder they released a free update giving you special weapons that are designed to carry you through the game and part way into the expansion? Most aspects of the game felt like a complete slog to get through to the point I took prolonged breaks. From long unskippable cutscenes to unnecessary padding quests as well as the multiplayer being very annoying in the story. As a long time fan of the series my biggest complaint was the boring weapon designs. Most of the weapons are just copy and pasted models with a different tuft of fur slapped to them as apposed to the unique weapons the PS2 or 3DS games had. When playing alone every fight felt long and boring to the point I would spend 15 to 30 minutes smacking around and chasing the monsters. Some quests I literally walked out on cuz I was just too drained to continue. 
This isn't a Monster Hunter problem by any means. I can hop in to MHTri on the Wii and have a great time even with underwater combat. Monster Hunter Wilds may have its flaws but at least I can take on boss monsters without needing to make a specialized speedrunner build. I will happily play Generations by myself for hours as I don't feel like I'm punching molasses during every quest. Ultimately, World puts me to sleep when not aggravating me with how much time I waste.
Game walls you right away with Great Jagras then every monster from then on is much easier... Broken monster order that should be switched up. But fun game though
Buy any other game if your planning on playing multiplayer. The matchmaking and UI mechanics are absolutely horrendous.

I used to enjoy this game but they have changed the fights so that the player can be stunlocked and attacked (usually killing you [Even with the best armor]) several times. Devs must have thought "Oh Players like mechanics that both make no sense, and works against them. 

The bs I have seen in the alatareon and fatalis make me not want to play another monster hunter game again which is sad because I started this series with the PSP a long time ago. But with their BS coupled with capcom selling out and also using denuvo, this is where our stories part ways. 
Goodbye monster hunter.
dont play this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game the event kulu ya ku is a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ aswell as the its creator

Very very very recommended, fatalis is a good exercise if you're having a bad day, and mewjang, we all love you both.

and, good luck with the tutorial phase for the new hunter in here, hope you all having fun.

This game is consistently infuriating, nauseating and genuinely headache inducing. I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hate this game.
But my love for the Charge Blade overpowers it all. If the Charge Blade has a million fans, I am one of them. If the Charge Blade has only ten fans, I am still one of them. If the Charge Blade has one fan, I am that fan. If the Charge Blade has no fans, I have killed myself. If the world is against the Charge Blade, then I am against the world. I want to make sweet, sweaty, raunchy, furious love with the Charge Blade. The Charge Blade can have my kids. 10 of them. 25. A whole new generation of ♥♥♥♥ sapiens would be born from such passionate coitus.
Oh and I hate The Handler. Stupid useless ♥♥♥♥♥.
Me hit big beast with big stick. Me happy, play with pal, very fun.

For Our September 21, 2026 Anniversary
Chicken and broccoli recipe

2 cans of cream of chicken
2 cups of mayonnaise
2lbs of broccoli
2lbs chicken breast

Start by cutting the chicken into strips
Fill a large pot of water and parboil the broccoli and the chicken
during this time mix the mayonnaise and cream of chicken together
when chicken is no longer pink and the broccoli is soft take out the ingredients and put it into a casserole pan
pre heat the oven to 375 for 30 mins

---------------------------------------------------------
● Honey do you have anything else to say to me?
-Honey, I know the chicken and broccoli you make is delicious.

As someone who has been playing this series since 4U, I was blown away by how rich and vibrant the world is in this game. The combat feels amazing, the story was cool, the monsters look awesome, and the endgame loop was a lot of fun. i highly recommend playing this game.
Best game to introduce newcomer to Monster Hunter series.
